description: Preflight inventory lists every remaining runtime `template_contract_id` reference in the targeted packages and classifies it in the scratchpad.
The plan explicitly states whether `client_contracts.template_contract_id` is provenance-only metadata or ready for future removal, with no ambiguous wording.
Unit/static coverage proves `applyContractToClient(...)` no longer derives template source with `template_contract_id ?? contract_id`.
DB-backed integration: applying a contract with valid provenance succeeds without any template fallback join or mixed runtime-ID source resolution.
DB-backed failure case: applying a contract that requires authoring provenance but lacks it fails with an explicit error instead of silently using `contract_id` as template source.
Unit/static coverage proves `packages/clients/src/actions/clientContractLineActions.ts` no longer uses `template_contract_id ?? contract_id` or equivalent mixed source fallback in line-add flows.
DB-backed integration: adding or cloning a client contract line succeeds from explicit authoring source data without template fallback reads.
DB-backed failure case: adding or cloning a client contract line with missing required provenance fails closed with a user-readable error.
Runtime client-contract line DTO/model tests prove `template_contract_id` is treated as provenance metadata only and not required for normal live reads.
Static or unit coverage proves `ContractLineService` setup/mutation code no longer infers template source from runtime contract IDs.
Focused API/service test proves contract-line setup still works when provenance exists and does not backfill or reinterpret runtime IDs when it does not.
Runtime contract loader tests prove template joins in `packages/billing/src/models/contract.ts` and related actions are provenance-only and do not widen instantiated contract lookup behavior.
Assignment/detail lookup tests prove runtime contract queries do not accept template IDs as interchangeable live identifiers after the cleanup.
Script-level contract test proves `server/scripts/contract-template-decoupling.ts` no longer backfills `template_contract_id` or preserves hybrid fallback semantics.
Shared type/schema tests prove `template_contract_id` fields that remain are documented and shaped as provenance-only metadata for callers.
Legacy tests/fixtures that previously asserted template fallback behavior are removed or updated to assert explicit failure/provenance semantics instead.

Static guard test fails if new runtime code in targeted packages/scripts introduces `template_contract_id ?? contract_id`, `coalesce(template_contract_id, contract_id)`, or comparable mixed-ID fallback patterns.
DB-backed integration sanity suite covers one happy path and one failure path for post-normalization contract application against migrated schema.
DB-backed integration sanity suite covers one happy path and one failure path for post-normalization client contract-line add/clone flows against migrated schema.
The scratchpad/runbook explicitly lists the remaining allowed provenance-only read surfaces so future reviewers can distinguish metadata reads from forbidden runtime behavior.
